codeReview.summary: buy-simulation.ts generally matches the requested reporting flow, but market-maker-test.ts has a blocking taker-fill flaw: it uses the same account for maker and taker while on-chain matching explicitly prevents self-trades, so the required fill simulation is not reliably achieved and success can be reported without an actual fill.

issues.description: The taker fill uses the same `account` that placed the ladder orders. The matching engine skips same-owner matches (`contracts/sources/matching.move:112` and `:192`), so this script cannot reliably fill against its own ladder and does not satisfy the required taker-fill simulation behavior.

`fillSuccess` is set from transaction success only, not from observed fills. Market orders can commit with zero trades (`contracts/sources/order_placement.move:386-387`), so the script can report success without any actual taker fill, making inventory/PnL results invalid for the feature intent.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The backend-worker skill mandates TDD for all tasks, but this feature is an integration-script task that depends on live chain credentials and state; the worker had to deviate.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/backend-worker/SKILL.md:28 requires "Write tests FIRST"; handoff notes deviation and rationale at mission handoff file lines 41-43 and suggested skill update at line 47.

sharedStateObservations.observation: New runnable scripts were added to scripts/package.json but not mirrored in shared command registry.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: scripts/package.json:16-17 includes `buy-simulation` and `market-maker-test`; .factory/services.yaml has `integration-test` and `performance-report` commands (lines 14 and 16) but no entries for the new scripts.

sharedStateObservations.observation: A critical protocol behavior (self-trade prevention) is not captured in shared library docs, which can lead to invalid simulation design choices.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: No `self-trade` mention in .factory/library (search returned no matches), while enforcement exists in contracts/sources/matching.move:112 and :192.

summary: FAIL — buy-simulation-script has blocking correctness issues in market-maker-test: taker-fill is implemented with the same account despite self-trade prevention, and success criteria do not verify that any fill actually occurred.
